page: Google Cloud Platform c4-highcpu-2 is a compute-optimized instance featuring 2 vCPUs and 4 GB of RAM, yielding a 1:2 vCPU-to-RAM ratio. The instance is powered by an Intel Xeon 8581C processor with an x86_64 architecture, operating at a base clock speed of 2.3 GHz. This single physical core configuration supports hyper-threading with two threads per core and provides dedicated CPU allocation. The processor includes a 260 MB L3 cache, 2 MB L2 cache, and 48 KB L1d cache, alongside advanced instruction sets such as AVX-512 and Intel Advanced Matrix Extensions (AMX). No local storage or GPUs are bundled with this instance.

Benchmark results indicate highly polarized performance characteristics. The instance excels in single-threaded tasks, achieving top-tier status in Geekbench single-core scores (2292.0) and single-threaded bzip3 decompression (14.76 GB/sec). Single-threaded gzip compression also registers strong performance at 34.76 GB/sec. Conversely, multi-core and memory-bound metrics are significantly limited. Multi-core performance in stress-ng (2216.77) and Geekbench (2816.0) falls into the weak category due to the low physical core count. Memory bandwidth is a notable bottleneck, with large-block read and write speeds ranking in the bottom 10% at 20.28 GB/sec and 14.17 GB/sec respectively.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, the c4-highcpu-2 offers a low-cost entry point into the dedicated Intel Xeon family, providing high single-core compute capabilities without the overhead of larger multi-core configurations. However, the low RAM capacity of 4 GB and poor memory bandwidth represent clear tradeoffs, making it unsuitable for memory-intensive applications or large-scale databases. This instance is best suited for lightweight, single-threaded compute workloads, basic text processing, simple web serving, and small-scale development or testing environments that benefit from dedicated CPU execution but do not require extensive memory or multi-core scaling.
description: Google Cloud Platform c4-highcpu-2 is a compute-optimized instance featuring 2 vCPUs and 4 GB of RAM, powered by an Intel Xeon 8581C processor running at 2.3 GHz. Built on the x86_64 architecture, it provides a single physical core with two threads and dedicated CPU allocation, but lacks local storage and GPU accelerators. Performance benchmarks reveal a stark contrast: the instance delivers top-tier single-core execution, highlighted by strong Geekbench single-core and single-threaded decompression scores, but exhibits weak multi-core performance and poor memory bandwidth. Qualitatively, it offers a cost-effective entry point for dedicated compute resources where memory demands are minimal. The c4-highcpu-2 is best suited for single-threaded processing tasks, lightweight web serving, and small-scale development workloads that do not require high memory density or multi-threaded scaling.
og_description: Google Cloud Platform c4-highcpu-2 is a compute-optimized instance with 2 vCPUs, 4 GB RAM, and an Intel Xeon 8581C CPU. It delivers strong single-core performance but has limited memory bandwidth and multi-core capacity.
meta_description: Google Cloud Platform c4-highcpu-2 is a 2-vCPU, 4 GB RAM compute-optimized instance powered by an Intel Xeon 8581C CPU with dedicated allocation.
tagline: A budget-friendly, compute-focused instance delivering strong single-core performance and dedicated CPU resources for lightweight, single-threaded workloads.
bullet_points: Powered by an Intel Xeon 8581C processor with 2 vCPUs and 4 GB RAM
Top-tier single-core performance in Geekbench and decompression benchmarks
Dedicated CPU allocation with AVX-512 and Intel AMX support
No bundled local storage or GPU accelerators
Best suited for lightweight single-threaded workloads and basic web serving
